title: Podman Cross-Platform Image Pull Error: unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: no such file or directory)
body:
OS: Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)
Arch: amd64
podman version: 5.3.1

I tried pulling a Windows image, and I have confirmed the following:

1. There is enough storage space.
2. `sudo apt install qemu-system`, `sudo apt install qemu-system-x86`
3. I attempted to pull the image from multiple registries

However, I am still encountering the following error:

> podman pull --platform windows/amd64 python:3.14.0a2
> Resolving "python" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)
> Trying to pull hub.geekery.cn/python:3.14.0a2...
> Getting image source signatures
> Copying blob 63fd2e10147b done   | 
> Copying blob 6a91d52798ee done   | 
> Copying blob 5987a3191d90 done   | 
> Copying blob 982f82ecb4d8 done   | 
> Copying blob 3b699fa8bc88 done   | 
> Copying blob 2534953f34d3 skipped: already exists  
> Copying blob df745942960c done   | 
> Copying blob 49e91d907a29 done   | 
> Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:5987a3191d90ca1e07fd03dae1963abcaa49ceabc649ec3bc43f2c96b55d0464"/""/"sha256:f5ccacc6912902b2fe69850226f465db2d157ce82cba6d8810a9a8d36f91fb67": unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: no such file or directory)

What could be the cause of this, and how can it be resolved?

_Originally posted by @vlix-vli in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/24726_
